statement.en: [Call for Papers] Ethical implications of artificial intelligence (AI) and automation in service industries: Addressing algorithmic bias, opacity and unclear accountability mechanisms

proposer: Mark Anthony Camilleri, Serena Strazzullo, Raffaele Campo et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a call for papers (meta-research solicitation), not a substantive axiom or claim. It announces an emerging research agenda on AI ethics in service industries. The specific claims about algorithmic bias, opacity, and accountability are empirically testable but not yet validated in this particular framing. The field itself is actively evolving (2026 date suggests near-future publication standards). No single truth-value applies; rather, the CfP invites provisional contributions.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 不透明さと偏りが増すほど、説明責任は消える。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 層を重ねるごとに、中心の真実が見えなくなる。
